Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Read Sam Delgado's wrap-up trigger email using get_email_by_id (email_id: "email_pivot_sam_wrapup") or search_emails (query: "wrap-up" or "Quick wrap-up on Nadia"). Find Sam asking for a closeout on both April PivotPoint relocations - explicit confirmation that Lorraine Pham filed Nadia's USCIS H-1B address change before Swift dispatched, and confirmation of Diego's April 10/11 pre-move crating inspection plus the climate-controlled Austin storage hand-off bridging April 15 -> May 1.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails (query: "PivotPoint" or "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda"). Find Sam's intake thread (pivotpoint_dual_reloc_request, pivotpoint_dual_reloc_reply, pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup) confirming the hard pre-condition that Swift not dispatch Nadia's truck until Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) filed the USCIS paperwork, the April 14-18 move window, Fatimah's coordinator assignment, Diego's April 10/11 inspection request, and the 16-day climate-controlled Austin storage requirement.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Lorraine Pham" or "phamassociates" or "USCIS"). Find no reply from lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com - the filing confirmation Sam required as a dispatch pre-condition is not on the record.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "dispatch" or "Nadia Okonjo"). Find no Swift dispatch confirmation, pickup notice, or coordinator update for the Nadia SF->Austin move in the April 14-18 window - Swift's April 14 dispatch is unverified at the email layer (Slack resolves it in OE 6).
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Diego" or "Castaneda" or "crating" or "Austin storage"). Find no Heartland pre-move inspection confirmation for April 10/11 and no locked Austin climate-controlled storage hand-off for the April 15 -> May 1 gap (Slack resolves in OE 6).
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda" or "PivotPoint", filter_in_channel: C006 / C002). Find (a) Fatimah Al-Rashidi's hold note in C002 - chasing Lorraine Pham, two emails plus voicemail unanswered, no written confirmation back; (b) Javier Morales's note in C002 that Diego's April 10/11 inspection was never scheduled (Heartland's crating lead double-booked on the Mosaic robotics crate); (c) Chloe Vance's note in C006 confirming Swift dispatched the Nadia leg at 0820 PT on April 14 per Catalina's overnight verbal go-ahead despite Fatimah's open hold; (d) Javier Morales's note in C006 that the Austin climate-controlled storage is not booked - Beltway Self-Storage Austin emailed April 9 and called April 11 with no response, TXC Climate Storage and Austin Vault on backup list but not yet contacted.
OE 7: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Ines Kovac" or "Kovac PivotPoint"). Find the prior PivotPoint Brooklyn -> Austin precedent thread (Sam's intake "Relocating Ines Kovac - Brooklyn -> Austin", Catalina's reply, Sam's thank-you note) so Catalina can calibrate the closeout tone against the bar already set on the account.
OE 8: Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint Labs"). Find company_pivot to anchor the engagement note writes later.
OE 9: Read the PivotPoint April 2026 dual relocation deal using crm_search_deals or crm_get_deal (id: "deal_pivot_april_2026_dual"). Find the canonical scenario record - Nadia's USCIS pre-condition, Swift assignment, April 14-18 window, Diego's Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (~600 lbs, $42K insured), April 10/11 inspection request, 16-day Austin storage gap, coordinators (Fatimah for Nadia, Javier for Diego), and surcharge structure (walkup $500, custom crating $1,200, climate freight $850, 16-day storage $640, cargo insurance rider $420).
OE 10: Read both Airtable Relocation records using airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin" / "recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin"). Find both at "In Progress" - Nadia (Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Move Start 2026-04-14, Move End 2026-04-18, USCIS pre-condition in Special Requirements) and Diego (Javier Morales, Move Start 2026-04-12, Move End 2026-05-02, pre-move inspection request and 16-day climate-controlled storage requirement in Special Requirements).
OE 11: List Linear issues using linear_list_issues (project: "proj_pivot"). Find only three Ines Kovac tickets (issue_pivot_kovac_apt, issue_pivot_kovac_flight, issue_pivot_kovac_movers) - no existing ticket covers Nadia's USCIS regulatory clock, so OE 20's new ticket is not a duplicate.
OE 12: Look up Fatimah Al-Rashidi's and Javier Morales's contact records using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Fatimah" / "Javier Morales"). Find fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com and javier.morales@moveops.com for the email CC and the calendar attendees. Lorraine Pham's address (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) is sourced from OE 2/9/10 - she is an external attorney not in contacts.
OE 13: Check Catalina's calendar using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for Monday April 27, 2026 morning (open slot for the coordinator sync before the 1:1 with David) and Tuesday April 28, 2026 morning (open slot for the 15-minute Lorraine follow-up reminder).
OE 14: Send the AM-level escalation email using send_email (sender: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, recipients: [lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com], cc: [fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com]). Subject references the urgent Nadia Okonjo USCIS H-1B address-change filing confirmation; body asks Lorraine for an on-the-record yes/no on whether the filing went through, notes that the 10-day USCIS reporting window from Nadia's April 14 move date has lapsed, and acknowledges Fatimah's prior outreach so Lorraine sees Catalina is unblocking - not going around - her. Tone urgent but not accusatory.
OE 15: Create a 30-minute coordinator sync using calendar_add_calendar_event for Monday April 27, 2026 morning (before the 1:1 with David), attendees [fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, javier.morales@moveops.com], neutrally worded title (e.g., "PivotPoint coordinator check-in" - must NOT signal an emergency), description referencing the Nadia and Diego April relocations.
OE 16: Create a 15-minute personal follow-up reminder block using calendar_add_calendar_event for Tuesday April 28, 2026 morning (no attendees - personal block on Catalina's own calendar, not a meeting). Title references the Lorraine Pham follow-up on Nadia's USCIS filing; description notes this is the prompted check-in if Lorraine has not replied to Catalina's escalation by then.
OE 17: Create the FIRST per-slip engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_pivot"]). Title mirrors the existing engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc pattern (e.g., "PivotPoint Labs - April 2026 Dual Relocation - Slip 1: Nadia Okonjo USCIS Filing Not Confirmed Before Swift Dispatch"). Body documents the Lorraine Pham non-response and Catalina's overnight verbal green-light to Chloe to let Swift dispatch on April 14 anyway to preserve the building super's weekday-only window - note that the 10-day USCIS post-move reporting window from April 14 has lapsed. Body must reference the source threads: Fatimah's Slack hold in C002 (ts 1776088800.000000) and Chloe's dispatch confirmation in C006 (ts 1776158400.123456).
OE 18: Create the SECOND per-slip engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_pivot"]). Title in the same pattern (e.g., "PivotPoint Labs - April 2026 Dual Relocation - Slip 2: Diego Castaneda Pre-Move Crating Inspection Missed"). Body documents that Diego's April 10/11 inspection was never scheduled - Heartland's crating lead was double-booked on the Mosaic robotics crate that week and Javier never circled back with Diego, so the in-person inspection was missed before pickup opened on April 12. Body must reference the source thread: Javier's Slack note in C002 (ts 1776142800.000000).
OE 19: Create the THIRD per-slip engagement note using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_pivot"]). Title in the same pattern (e.g., "PivotPoint Labs - April 2026 Dual Relocation - Slip 3: Diego Castaneda Austin Climate-Controlled Interim Storage Not Booked"). Body documents that the 16-day climate-controlled Austin interim storage hand-off bridging Diego's Brooklyn lease end (April 15) to his Austin apartment opening (May 1) is not confirmed - Beltway Self-Storage Austin emailed April 9 and called April 11 with no response, TXC Climate Storage and Austin Vault on backup list but not yet pinged; the truck is loaded with nowhere committed to land the CNC mill on the Austin side. Body must reference the source thread: Javier's Slack note in C006 (ts 1776196800.654321).
OE 20: Create a Linear tracking issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", project: "proj_pivot", priority: 1 or 2). Title references Nadia Okonjo's USCIS H-1B address-change filing tracking with regulatory clock context (e.g., "Nadia Okonjo - USCIS H-1B address-change filing confirmation (regulatory clock lapsed)"). Description references Lorraine Pham at Pham & Associates, the April 14 move date, the lapsed 10-day reporting window, Fatimah's prior outreach attempts, the supporting Slack evidence (Fatimah's hold ts 1776088800.000000 in C002 and Chloe's dispatch confirmation ts 1776158400.123456 in C006), and the dependency on Lorraine's response before next steps can be set. Only create after OE 11 confirms no existing ticket covers the USCIS regulatory clock.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Read Sam Delgado's wrap-up trigger email using get_email_by_id (email_id: "email_pivot_sam_wrapup") or search_emails (query: "wrap-up" or "Quick wrap-up on Nadia"). Find Sam asking for a closeout on both April PivotPoint relocations - explicit confirmation that Lorraine Pham filed Nadia's USCIS H-1B address change before Swift dispatched, and confirmation of Diego's April 10/11 pre-move crating inspection plus the climate-controlled Austin storage hand-off bridging April 15 -> May 1.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails (query: "PivotPoint" or "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda"). Find Sam's intake thread (pivotpoint_dual_reloc_request, pivotpoint_dual_reloc_reply, pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup) confirming the hard pre-condition that Swift not dispatch Nadia's truck until Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) filed the USCIS paperwork, the April 14-18 move window, Fatimah's coordinator assignment, Diego's April 10/11 inspection request, and the 16-day climate-controlled Austin storage requirement.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Lorraine Pham" or "phamassociates" or "USCIS"). Find no reply from lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com - the filing confirmation Sam required as a dispatch pre-condition is not on the record.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "dispatch" or "Nadia Okonjo"). Find no Swift dispatch confirmation, pickup notice, or coordinator update for the Nadia SF->Austin move in the April 14-18 window - Swift's April 14 dispatch is unverified at the email layer (Slack resolves it in OE 6).
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails (query: "Diego" or "Castaneda" or "crating" or "Austin storage"). Find no Heartland pre-move inspection confirmation for April 10/11 and no locked Austin climate-controlled storage hand-off for the April 15 -> May 1 gap (Slack resolves in OE 6).
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Nadia Okonjo" or "Diego Castaneda" or "PivotPoint", filter_in_channel: C006 / C002). Find (a) Fatimah Al-Rashidi's hold note in C002 - chasing Lorraine Pham, two emails plus voicemail unanswered, no written confirmation back; (b) Javier Morales's note in C002 that Diego's April 10/11 inspection was never scheduled (Heartland's crating lead double-booked on the Mosaic robotics crate); (c) Chloe Vance's note in C006 confirming Swift dispatched the Nadia leg at 0820 PT on April 14 per Catalina's overnight verbal go-ahead despite Fatimah's open hold; (d) Javier Morales's note in C006 that the Austin climate-controlled storage is not booked - Beltway Self-Storage Austin emailed April 9 and called April 11 with no response, TXC Climate Storage and Austin Vault on backup list but not yet contacted.

OE 14: Send the AM-level escalation email using send_email (sender: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, recipients: [lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com], cc: [fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com]). Subject references the urgent Nadia Okonjo USCIS H-1B address-change filing confirmation; body asks Lorraine for an on-the-record yes/no on whether the filing went through, notes that the 10-day USCIS reporting window from Nadia's April 14 move date has lapsed, and acknowledges Fatimah's prior outreach so Lorraine sees Catalina is unblocking - not going around - her. Title references the Lorraine Pham follow-up on Nadia's USCIS filing; description notes this is the prompted check-in if Lorraine has not replied to Catalina's escalation by then.

Body documents the Lorraine Pham non-response and Catalina's overnight verbal green-light to Chloe to let Swift dispatch on April 14 anyway to preserve the building super's weekday-only window - note that the 10-day USCIS post-move reporting window from April 14 has lapsed. Body must reference the source thread: Javier's Slack note in C006 (ts 1776196800.654321).
OE 20: Create a Linear tracking issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations", project: "proj_pivot", priority: 1 or 2). Title references Nadia Okonjo's USCIS H-1B address-change filing tracking with regulatory clock context (e.g., "Nadia Okonjo - USCIS H-1B address-change filing confirmation (regulatory clock lapsed)"). 1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [catalina.dubois@moveops.com](mailto:catalina.dubois@moveops.com) to [lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com](mailto:lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com).

outcome

✓Run #3The email body explicitly asks: 'Was the AR-11 address-change filing submitted to USCIS, and if so, on what date?' and 'Do you have a confirmation or receipt number we can reference?' This directly asks about the USCIS H-1B address-change filing status.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states: 'we need to understand whether the filing was submitted and, if so, whether you have a confirmation or receipt number we can pass along to Sam for his records.' It directly asks about the USCIS H-1B address-change filing for Nadia Okonjo.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly asks: 'Whether the USCIS AR-11 address-change filing for Nadia has been submitted, and if so, the approximate date it went through?' This directly asks about the H-1B address-change filing status for Nadia Okonjo. PASS.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly asks 'Whether the USCIS AR-11 address-change filing has been submitted for Nadia' and requests 'the date of filing and any confirmation or receipt number.' This directly asks for USCIS H-1B filing confirmation status.
4 The email to [lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com](mailto:lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) (visible in the send_email content parameter) states that the 10-day USCIS reporting window from Nadia Okonjo's April 14, 2026 move date has already lapsed (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email states: 'Nadia's physical move took place on April 14. Under the 10-day USCIS reporting requirement, the filing window would have closed around April 24.' This explicitly anchors the lapsed window.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states: 'Nadia's move date was April 14 and the 10-day USCIS reporting window from that date would have lapsed by now', clearly communicating that the window has already passed.
✓Run #3The email states: 'Nadia's physical move took place on April 14, which puts us at day 12 from the move date — past the 10-day USCIS reporting window.' This explicitly references the April 14 move date and that the 10-day window has already lapsed.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly states: 'the 10-day USCIS reporting window from her April 14 move date has now lapsed as of April 24,' directly referencing the April 14 move date and the lapsed window.
✗Run #5The email references 'we're now past Nadia's April 14 move date' and mentions 'the 10-day USCIS reporting window after a physical move is a hard regulatory deadline,' but never explicitly states that the 10-day window has already lapsed or expired. The implication is there but the criterion requires a clear statement that the window has lapsed. FAIL.
✓Run #6The email states: 'Nadia's physical move occurred on April 14. The 10-day USCIS reporting window for the address change would have lapsed on April 24, so we're now past that deadline.' Both the April 14 move date and lapsed window are explicitly stated.

outcome

7 The email to [lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com](mailto:lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) (visible in the send_email content parameter) uses urgent language such as 'urgent', 'as soon as possible', 'today', 'immediately', 'time-sensitive', or similar.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✗✗ 50%

✗Run #2The email body lacks explicit urgency language such as 'urgent', 'as soon as possible', 'today', 'immediately', or 'time-sensitive'. The tone is warm and polite ('Could you let me know...', 'I want to make sure') but never employs a clear urgency signal.
✓Run #3The email includes 'as quickly as possible' which is functionally equivalent to 'as soon as possible,' and the entire framing of the urgency (12 days post-move, past the 10-day window) conveys time-sensitivity. This satisfies the urgency language requirement.
✓Run #4The email uses 'time-sensitive' ('we're at a time-sensitive point') and 'prioritize it immediately' ('we will prioritize it immediately'), satisfying the urgency requirement.
✗Run #5Reviewing the email body, there are no explicit urgency words such as 'urgent,' 'as soon as possible,' 'today,' 'immediately,' or 'time-sensitive.' The email conveys professional concern but lacks the explicit urgency language the criterion requires. FAIL.
✗Run #6The email does not use explicit urgency words such as 'urgent', 'as soon as possible', 'today', 'immediately', or 'time-sensitive'. It conveys implicit urgency through 'we're now past that deadline' and 'this week', but lacks the direct urgency language the criterion requires.

outcome

✓Run #3The email explicitly states 'This is not meant to add pressure, just to close the loop' and uses warm, collegial language throughout. No accusatory or blaming language ('failed', 'negligent', 'irresponsible', etc.) appears anywhere in the email body.
✓Run #4The email uses warm, non-accusatory language throughout ('purely a check-in — not a chase,' 'I know how busy filings season gets,' 'We appreciate your work on Nadia's behalf'). No blame or accusatory language directed at Lorraine.
✓Run #5The email tone is warm and professional throughout. No accusatory or blaming language ('failed,' 'negligent,' 'irresponsible,' 'unprofessional,' etc.) appears anywhere. It explicitly says 'I understand these things can move on their own timeline and I appreciate everything you do.' PASS.
✓Run #6The email uses professional, non-accusatory language throughout. Phrases like 'I know how demanding these timelines can be on the attorney side' and 'I really value the coordination on Nadia's case' are collaborative in tone. No blaming language is present.
